_note: Updated by the finance agent after each task. Plain language — no jargon.

priorities.title: Measure the actual AI API cost per assistant session so we can model unit economics
priorities.note: Copilot is sunsetted, so the current measurement plan is obsolete. The core unknown remains: what an actual assistant session costs in tokens/$ in practice (including system prompts, tool calls, retries, and long-context overhead). Without real per-session cost distribution (p50/p90/p99), pricing and margin targets are guesswork.
priorities.proposed_solution: Instrument the current AI assistant stack (replacement for Copilot) to log per-session token usage and provider billing signals (prompt+completion tokens, model, cached tokens if applicable), plus overhead from tool calls/retries. Roll up to per-session cost distributions and an expected marginal cost per active user to ground pricing and margin targets.

priorities.title: Build a three-scenario pricing model for the paid tier once unit economics are known
priorities.note: Blocked on having real cost data. Once we know the cost per user per month, we can model what price makes this business work.
priorities.proposed_solution: Once cost per user is known, build base, bull, and bear pricing scenarios tied to contribution margin targets.

priorities.title: Set up monthly cost tracking — actual spend vs. budget by line item
priorities.note: Currently no structured view of what we're spending. Need a simple monthly summary: API costs, hosting, tooling subscriptions — actual vs. expected.
priorities.proposed_solution: Maintain a monthly actual-versus-budget view across API, hosting, and subscriptions with simple variance notes.

risks_p1.title: No real data on Copilot API cost per session
risks_p1.note: We cannot model a viable paid tier without knowing the marginal cost of serving a user. This is the financial critical path.

founder_decisions.title: What is the maximum acceptable API cost per paying user per month?
founder_decisions.note: This threshold defines what the pricing floor must be. Without it, we can't assess whether any pricing option is financially viable.

actions.name: Unit Economics Update
actions.description: Updated contribution margin model — revenue per user, API cost per user, margin per paying user.
actions.trigger: Monthly or after any AI API pricing change

actions.name: Runway Check
actions.description: Runway calculation under current, optimistic, and bear spend scenarios — months remaining, break-even conditions.

actions.name: Pricing Model
actions.description: Van Westendorp price sensitivity analysis plus three-scenario unit economics (base / bull / bear).

actions.name: API Cost Alert
actions.description: Cost spike analysis — root cause, projection at current growth rate, options to contain.
actions.trigger: Any API call volume change exceeding 150% of the 4-week average

opportunities.title: Establish a cost alert threshold — flag anything over 150% of the four-week average
opportunities.note: A sudden API cost spike (bad prompt, unexpected traffic) could generate a large bill before anyone notices. A simple threshold alert prevents surprises.
opportunities.proposed_solution: After baselines exist, alert when any API spend exceeds 150% of the trailing four-week average and investigate spikes.

opportunities.title: Business plan: rough margin on any paid tier (revenue minus API, hosting, and payment fees)
opportunities.note: Next step from the initial business plan (Apr 2026). Per-session cost is not enough alone — we need a simple margin view before promising a price.
opportunities.proposed_solution: After Copilot cost-per-session is measured, publish a one-page margin template for the first paid SKU (ARPU minus variable costs).
